_description: OVERALL TIER CAP (§6.4): overall tier cannot exceed Expert unless ≥4 dims are Proficient+ AND ≥2 are Expert.
_key_rule: Overall tier cannot exceed Expert unless ≥4 dimensions are individually Proficient+ AND ≥2 are Expert. If the constraint fails, overall score is capped at 7.5.

case_A_cap_applied_only_1_expert._description: Average score would be Expert range, but only 1 Expert dimension (and 3 Proficient) → capped at 7.5.

overall._note: Mean is 6.1 (Proficient) — cap would only apply if overall WOULD be Expert. 6.1 < 7.6 so no cap needed here.

case_B_cap_applied_high_mean_but_insuffi._description: High mean in Expert range (7.8) but only 1 Expert dim — must cap at 7.5.

dimensions._wait: Actually need ≥2 Expert dims but let's test with mean that yields Expert but only 1 Expert dim

expected._note: SKIP — duplicate D2 makes this invalid. See case_C below.

case_C_cap_applied_1_expert_5_proficient._description: 1 Expert dim + 5 Proficient dims. Mean may be in Expert range. Cap check: ≥4 Proficient+ (5 Proficient + 1 Expert = 6 ≥4 ✓) but only 1 Expert (need ≥2 ✗) → cap at 7.5.

overall._cap_check: ≥4 Proficient+: 6 dims ✓. ≥2 Expert: only 1 ✗. Constraint FAILS → cap at 7.5.

case_D_no_cap_4_proficient_plus_2_expert._description: 4 Proficient+ dims and 2 Expert dims → constraint passes → no cap applied.

overall._note: Mean 6.1 is Proficient anyway — but constraint also passes (4 Proficient+: D1,D2,D3,D4; 2 Expert: D1,D2).

case_E_no_cap_passes_both_expert_gates._description: 6 Expert dims, all very high → both constraint gates pass → no cap, score can be Expert.

overall._note: ≥4 Proficient+ (all 6) ✓. ≥2 Expert (all 6) ✓. Constraint passes, score is Expert.
